mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for agentic workflow development, designed to facilitate the creation and management of AI-driven applications. It is ideal for teams looking to leverage AI capabilities in their workflows while maintaining control over their infrastructure. Key trade-offs include the need for self-hosting versus using Dify Cloud, which may impact resource management and operational overhead.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dify Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dify using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.dify.ai

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Dify Helm repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
Before installing Dify, we need to install the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for its operation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Dify application in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace '0.12.0' with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for Dify to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample Dify application.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dify application to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get difyapp -n dify

```

> This command lists the Dify applications in the 'dify' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dify application and its associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss for any applications created under Dify.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This command uninstalls the Dify application.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Dify resources. This step will upgrade Dify to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get difyapp -n dify -o yaml > difyapp-backup.yaml

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.1

kubectl get difyapp -n dify

```

> Backup the current Dify application configuration.
> Replace '0.12.1' with the desired version.
> Verify that the Dify application is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get difyapp -n dify -o yaml > difyapp-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'DifyApp not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Dify application does not exist in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Dify application name is correct and that it has been created.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs may not be applied correctly due to network issues or incorrect URLs.

**Fix:**
Re-run the CRD installation command and check for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be insufficient resources or a misconfiguration in the Helm chart.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm chart values and ensure the cluster has enough resources.

troubleshooting.title: Dify application is not responding
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dify application pod may be in a crash loop or not running.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n dify' and describe the pod for logs.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API endpoints for application management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your application configurations to use the new API endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify application listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get difyapp -n dify'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
